rationale: The repository is small (~4.3k LOC of scripts) with high cohesion around a single Codex runtime layer and one dominant workflow: writing and extending node:test coverage, verified by `npm test`. There is no evidence of independent, high-cohesion domain areas that would justify more than one persistent agent, so this proposal follows the 'prefer exactly one persistent agent plus temporary-templates for small repositories' rule instead of a domain or layer split.

agents.name: Test and Verification Worker
responsibilities: Extend and maintain node:test coverage for new and existing orchestration modules
Verify every change against `npm test` before reporting a task as complete
Follow this repository's existing fixture-over-mock testing style
agents.rationale: Owns real, recurring work (test coverage), has independently verifiable output (`npm test`), and its write scope can be restricted to `tests/**` only — meets all four conditions for a persistent agent.

skillDrafts.purpose: Write and extend tests using Node's built-in `node:test` runner with zero test-framework dependencies.
useWhen: Adding coverage for a new or changed module under plugins/codex/scripts/**
procedure: Import assertions from `node:assert/strict` and the test runner from `node:test`
Prefer fixtures (e.g. `tests/fake-codex-fixture.mjs`) over mocks for external processes
verificationSteps: `npm test` passes with the new/changed test file included

skillDrafts.purpose: Follow this repository's specific test file naming, structure and helper conventions.
useWhen: Any test file added or modified in this repository
procedure: Reuse `tests/helpers.mjs` for shared setup (temp dirs, PATH shims) instead of duplicating it
Use `tests/fake-codex-fixture.mjs` when a test needs to fake the `codex` binary or app-server protocol
verificationSteps: New test file matches the naming and helper-reuse conventions above

overlaps: None: a single persistent agent owns tests/**, with read-only secondary access to plugins/codex/scripts/** for context.
risks: A single persistent agent means all worker-tier work funnels through one identity; if task volume grows, revisit the one-agent decision.
The worker model (DeepSeek-class) may produce invalid tool calls or loop on ambiguous test failures — mitigated by the bounded review loop and low maxToolCalls.
notes: See docs/MVP_PLAN.md §2 for the design rationale this proposal mirrors.
